mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0%, tight labor markets accelerate wage growth, increasing corporate profit margins and driving positive equity returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative or flat when UNRATE drops below 4.0% in >60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising housing starts signals robust aggregate demand, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in INDPRO and HOUST do not predict positive 60d SPX returns in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, rising core inflation expectations compress real yields and trigger equity repricing, but only when the federal funds rate exceeds 1.0%.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain positive when both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS rise above thresholds during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales and financial assets held by savings institutions reflect expanding consumer spending power, which positively drives corporate earnings and equity returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS momentum fails to predict positive SPX 20d returns in >50% of rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index signals supply chain bottlenecks or weakening manufacturing demand, triggering negative equity returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive when INDPRO contracts by >0.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in nonfarm payrolls and real GDP indicates synchronized economic expansion, which historically precedes positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive surprises in PAYEMS and GDPC1 do not yield positive 60d SPX returns in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts exceed 1.3 million units annually, it signals sustained construction activity and consumer confidence, driving positive equity returns over a 20-day horizon, but only during periods of low unemployment.
failure_condition: High HOUST fails to predict positive SPX 20d returns when UNRATE is below 4.5% in walk-forward validation.
